page: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Daldv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 2 physical AMD EPYC 9V74 cores with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.7 GHz. The processor includes 32 KB L1d, 1.0 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_VNNI, AVX512_BF16, and cryptographic instructions like AES and SHA_NI. The server is configured with 8.0 GB of system memory, providing 2.0 GB per core, and includes a 236 GB local disk with Premium Storage capabilities. No GPU resources are included.

Benchmark data shows strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and Geekbench single-core scores in the strong category (top 25%). However, multi-core performance is average, as seen in the stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core results. Memory subsystem performance represents a significant tradeoff; both small-block and large-block read-only memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the weak category (bottom 25%), and the Passmark memory score is similarly weak. This memory bottleneck impacts memory-intensive tasks, resulting in weak performance for software compilation and database operations.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the D4alds_v6 offers a low-memory, dedicated compute profile that minimizes memory overhead costs for workloads that do not require large RAM footprints. It performs adequately on standard web serving, showing average static web serving throughput and average Redis SET operations. The server is suited for single-threaded processing, text processing, and lightweight web serving. It is less suited for memory-bound databases, software compilation, or medium-to-large language model inference, where memory bandwidth and capacity constraints limit throughput.
description: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is an x86_64 server in the Daldv6 family, utilizing 4 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz. It is configured with 8.0 GB of memory (2.0 GB per core) and a 236 GB local disk. The server demonstrates top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong text processing capabilities. However, its memory subsystem presents a clear tradeoff, with read-only memory bandwidth and Passmark memory scores falling into the bottom 25%. This low-memory configuration offers qualitative cost efficiency for compute-focused tasks that do not require large RAM capacities. The D4alds_v6 is suited for single-threaded applications, text processing, and lightweight web serving, but is constrained in memory-intensive workloads like software compilation and database operations.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is an AMD EPYC-powered server featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs, 8.0 GB of RAM, and 236 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core CPU performance with a low-memory footprint.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D4alds_v6 is an AMD EPYC server with 4 vCPUs, 8.0 GB RAM, and 236 GB local storage, optimized for single-core compute workloads.
tagline: A dedicated AMD EPYC compute instance featuring strong single-core performance, local storage, and a low-memory footprint for targeted workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 9V74 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs at 3.7 GHz
8.0 GB system memory and 236 GB local storage
Top-tier single-core CPU and strong text processing performance
Weak read-only memory bandwidth and database performance tradeoffs
Suited for single-threaded tasks, text processing, and light web serving
